title: hieracium murorum subsp. incrassans
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. incrassans, also known as Wall Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its sap and leaves. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as skin conditions, wounds, and eye problems, due to its antibacterial and antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, Hieracium murorum subsp. incrassans is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, or salves to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various ailments.
